paper.title: Valorization of coarse rigid polyurethane foam waste in lightweight aggregate concrete
paper.authors: Amor Ben Fraj, Mohamed Kismi, Pierre Mounanga
paper.material_system: Lightweight aggregate concrete incorporating coarse rigid polyurethane foam waste as aggregate
paper.notes: Mixture proportions are available from Table 4. Compressive strength values are plotted in Fig. 8 but numeric plotted values are not readable from the supplied packet.

strength_results.notes: Fig. 8 gives 28-day compressive strength results, but the packet does not include readable numeric plotted values for NWC.
Abstract/conclusions report lightweight concrete compressive strength range of 8-16 MPa; mixture-level value requires Fig. 8 digitization.
Text states pre-wetting decreased compressive strength by 41% between LWAC-1 and LWAC-1sat, but exact mixture-level values require Fig. 8 digitization.

conditions.applies_to: all concrete cylindrical specimens before compressive-strength testing
conditions.rh_pct: >90 for first 24 h; water tank after demoulding
conditions.curing_method: moist-cured for 24 h, demoulded, then cylindrical specimens kept in a water tank until testing

conditions.applies_to: 28-day compressive strength of all concrete mixtures
conditions.test_method: compressive strength tested on cylindrical specimens; average of two specimens

figures.caption: Fig. 8 gives the results of compressive strength tests at 28 days.
figures.notes: Packet text references Fig. 8 but does not include readable numeric plotted values.

figures.caption: Values of dynamic modulus of elasticity plotted on Figs. 8 and 10 as a function of air-dried density.
figures.strength_relevance: secondary; may include Fig. 8 context but packet text focuses on dynamic modulus
figures.notes: Not used for direct compressive-strength extraction.

evidence.source_location: Section 2.1.1 Cement, normal weight aggregates and superplasticizer
evidence.evidence_text: The cement used is CEM I 52.5 N. A polycarboxylate-based superplasticizer was added to concretes with lower water-to-cement ratio.
The lightweight aggregates incorporated in concrete are coarse particles (8-20 mm) of PUR foam waste.
evidence.supports: PUR foam aggregate identity and size

evidence.evidence_text: Mix proportions (kg/m3): NWC 397 cement, 220 water, 824 sand, 867 normal aggregates, W/C 0.55; LWAC-1 397, 220, 824, PUR foam 15.1, W/C 0.55; LWAC-1sat same; LWAC-2sat 415, 183, 862, PUR foam 15.8, SP 1.405, W/C 0.44; LWAC-3sat 353, 156, 734, PUR foam 20.1, SP 1.196, W/C 0.44.
Four lightweight aggregate concretes (LWAC-1, LWAC-1sat, LWAC-2sat and LWAC-3sat), incorporating PUR-foam aggregates, and a normal weight concrete (NWC), as control material, were prepared.
Pre-wetting of LWA represents an addition of water of about 47 kg/m3, 49 kg/m3 and 62 kg/m3 for LWAC-1sat, LWAC-2sat and LWAC-3sat mixes, respectively.
evidence.supports: additional saturation water for prewetted lightweight aggregate mixtures

evidence.source_location: Section 2.3 Experimental program and testing procedures
evidence.evidence_text: Cylindrical specimens (diameter 11 cm; height 22 cm) were moist-cured at 20 +/- 2 C and over 90% RH for 24 h, demoulded, and kept in a water tank at 20 +/- 2 C until testing.
The 28-day compressive strength was tested on cylindrical specimens. Each mechanical property value is the average from tests on two specimens.
evidence.supports: compressive-strength age, specimen type, and replicate basis

evidence.evidence_text: Fig. 8 gives the results of compressive strength tests at 28 days. Lightweight concrete strengths decreased from 57% to 78% compared to the control concrete.
evidence.supports: Fig. 8 contains 28-day compressive strength values but packet lacks exact numeric plotted values

evidence.evidence_text: The mechanical properties of the LWAC ranged between 8 and 16 MPa for the compressive strength.
evidence.supports: range of lightweight concrete compressive strengths

evidence.evidence_text: The lightweight concretes showed compressive strength ranging between 8 and 16 MPa.
evidence.supports: conclusion-level range of lightweight concrete compressive strengths

review_queue.question: Digitize or manually read Fig. 8 to obtain 28-day compressive strength values in MPa for NWC, LWAC-1, LWAC-1sat, LWAC-2sat, and LWAC-3sat.
review_queue.suggested_action: Open the source PDF figure and record exact or digitized bar values; then update final_value, estimated_error_mpa, value_status, and human_decision for each strength result.
review_queue.notes: Packet text confirms Fig. 8 contains the target values, but no readable numeric values are provided.

main_risks: Compressive-strength values are not directly readable from the supplied packet and require Fig. 8 digitization.
The abstract and conclusions provide only a range for lightweight concretes, not mixture-level values.
PUR-foam volume content is captured in notes because the component amount field uses the kg/m3 mix-proportion values from Table 4.
